sections.paragraph:
ἀντίψυχον, ου, τό (s. ψυχή) ransom (cp. 4 Macc 6:29; 17:21. The adj. ἀντίψυχος, ον=someth. given in return for sparing one’s life: Lucian, Lex. 10 χρήματα ἀντίψυχα διδόναι; Cass. Dio 59, 8, 3 ἀντίψυχοι.) IEph 21:1; ISm 10:2; IPol 2:3; 6:1.—TW. Sv.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

adj. adj. = adjective

someth. someth. = something

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 91
